trivia_bullets.text: Cases are a new enchantment subtype debuting in Murders at Karlov Manor (released 2024-02-09). Each Case has three abilities: an always-on enter effect, a "To solve" condition checked at the beginning of your end step, and a "Solved" reward that activates once the condition is met. The mechanic literalizes the detective-procedural conceit of the set — a case that advances stage by stage until it's closed. [Scryfall] [Wizards: Murders at Karlov Manor Mechanics]

Case of the Shattered Pact has the most demanding solve condition in the Case cycle: five colors of permanents simultaneously on the battlefield. This gates it firmly in WUBRG (five-color) decks — primarily five-color Commander builds. Once solved, the reward (flying + double strike + vigilance on a creature each combat) is powerful but the solve condition means it rarely appears outside dedicated rainbow builds. [Scryfall]

As card no. 1 in the set, Case of the Shattered Pact holds the numerically first slot in Murders at Karlov Manor — a white enchantment that ramps (basic land on entry) while demanding the full chromatic spectrum to unlock its reward. The shattered-crystal art (iridescent shards in a dark void, by artist Peter Polach) visualizes a broken covenant or agreement — fitting for an Orzhov-adjacent set where the Karlov pact and guild compacts are under threat. [Scryfall]

Format legality is broad: legal in Standard, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander as of release. The card sees minimal competitive play — its solve condition is too demanding for most two- or three-color formats, and in Commander it competes with more efficient ramp spells. [Scryfall]
